fullArticle:
Agatha Christie’s literary legacy is unparalleled. A prolific murder mystery writer of phenomenal and enduring popularity, Christie arguably had a bigger hand than anyone else in making the genre what it is today, and her books have sold in the billions. They’ve also been adapted countless times into movies and TV shows across the globe.

Yet, it may have escaped the attention of Christie’s millions of devoted fans that the best screen adaptation of her work ever made is now streamable on one of the world’s biggest television platforms. This series is universally lauded for its faithful and comprehensive rendering of the author’s stories.

Moreover, its lively acting performances and peerless production values have never been topped. The show in question is, of course, ITV’s Poirot, which ...
